item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably my favourite SCP ever…promise I won't forget it <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Targets affected by SCP-3298 frequently report feelings of melancholy and loneliness; however, this does not significantly differ from control subjects in comparable non-anomalous situations,

Upvoted for feels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I never saw the username punchline, but the current ending feels strongly enough for me.

I have a thing lately for friendly and well-behaved anomalies, and I resonate a bit with this one, so thank you for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't entirely sure about the stinger myself, and the majority dislike of it has convinced me to remove it. Some minor edits have also been made for flow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought this was pretty solid, but the "letter addressed to the reader" bit at the end didn't really tug at my heartstrings. I've seen it done a few times, and I see it not being particularly effective for anyone whose username isn't a "normal" name. I'm afraid that's pushing me to novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice work. I have to admit, the stinger did get me at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
don't you
forget about me
don't don't don't don't

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wiiiiill remember youuuuu! Will you remeeeeeeember me?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This still puts 3298 leagues ahead of my average OKCupid encounter. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually I quite liked the username stinger, but mine is based on my real-world nickname so may have been more effective as a result.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found myself confused by the long and detailed description, so that I was rather put out by the time I got to the addenda. The characterization started bringing me back around, this anomaly being a person who craves human interaction while at the same time being terrified of and very bad at it. But the name gimmick at the end does not work, I'm sorry, it deflated any good graces I'd built up by that point. Novote, would revisit after a thorough rework.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that listpage became a bit underwhelming since it took me a second to recognize that it's my username—there's also the fact wiki usernames don't usually correlate to irl names, but it is nice article. Made me remember all those "what if"s that I left behind long ago. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't much like the ListPages stinger at the end, since I was expecting something along those lines, but apart from that I like the atmosphere of this piece a lot. Love liminal spaces, love missed connections; some part of my headcanon says it's Miriam Wells having a small reprieve from her private hell.

Upvoted.

item.title: 'Miss Connection'
item.content:encoded:
Dedicated to a stranger. (no relation to the image or description in the SCP)

Thanks to Presque for reading the draft.

Image source.
